goal: is_order_dual_iso_pair[G, G](neg_map[G], neg_map[G]) = (forall(x0: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x0)) = x0 } and forall(x1: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x1)) = x1 } and is_antitone[G, G](neg_map[G]) and is_antitone[G, G](neg_map[G]))
proof: function[T0: PartialOrder, T1: PartialOrder](x0: T1 -> T0, x1: T0 -> T1) { (forall(x2: T0) { x0(x1(x2)) = x2 } and forall(x3: T1) { x1(x0(x3)) = x3 } and is_antitone[T0, T1](x1) and is_antitone[T1, T0](x0)) = is_order_dual_iso_pair[T0, T1](x1, x0) }[G, G](neg_map[G], neg_map[G])
not (forall(x0: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x0)) = x0 } and forall(x1: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x1)) = x1 } and is_antitone[G, G](neg_map[G]) and is_antitone[G, G](neg_map[G]))
not (forall(x2: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x2)) = x2 } and forall(x3: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x3)) = x3 } and is_antitone[G, G](neg_map[G]))
not (forall(x4: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x4)) = x4 } and forall(x5: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x5)) = x5 })
let w0: G satisfy { neg_map(neg_map(w0)) != w0 }
function(x0: G) { neg_map(neg_map(x0)) = x0 }(w0)

goal: exists(k0: OrderDualIso[G, G]) { OrderDualIso.new[G, G](neg_map[G], neg_map[G]) = Option.some(k0) }
proof: function[T0: PartialOrder, T1: PartialOrder](x0: T0 -> T1, x1: T1 -> T0) { not OrderDualIso.constraint[T0, T1](x0, x1) or exists(k0: OrderDualIso[T0, T1]) { Option.some[OrderDualIso[T0, T1]](k0) = OrderDualIso.new[T0, T1](x0, x1) } }[G, G](neg_map[G], neg_map[G])
